message:
fix(net): clamp UDP recv stream buffer to 1 byte minimum; mirror empty WebSocket close payload

UDP RecvStream::new now clamps buf_size to at least 1 byte. A zero-length
receive buffer would cause recvfrom to consume and discard a queued
datagram while yielding an empty payload, silently dropping the entire
datagram body. The clamp ensures at least the first byte is captured.
Test added for the zero-buffer edge case.

WebSocket CloseHandshake::receive_close now mirrors the peer's close
frame payload shape: if the peer sends a close frame without a status
code (empty payload), the response close frame also has no status code
instead of synthesizing CloseCode::Normal. This aligns with RFC 6455
Section 7.1.6 which states the close frame response should echo the
peer's close frame structure. Test added for empty-close-payload mirroring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
